name: Community Action Partnership Of North Alabama - Moulton Branch
description_short: Community Action Partnership of North Alabama provides free or low-cost human services including utility assistance, housing counseling, childcare, and meals for low-income families across 16 North Alabama counties.
description_long:
Community Action Partnership of North Alabama (CAPNA) is a non-profit human services organization serving 16 counties in North Alabama including Morgan, Lawrence, Cullman, Marion, Winston, Lauderdale, Madison, Limestone, Walker, Colbert, Franklin, Jackson, Marshall, Blount, Cherokee, and DeKalb. The organization is part of the national Community Action Agency network and operates under the philosophy that 'Human Services That Treat You Like A Human.' CAPNA's Moulton Branch is one of their service delivery locations.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of free and subsidized services designed to help low-income individuals and families achieve self-sufficiency. These include energy assistance programs (utility bill help, weatherization), children's services (Head Start early childhood programs), housing counseling and affordable housing support, meals on wheels delivery for seniors and disabled individuals, and volunteer opportunities including Foster Grandparents and Senior Companions programs. They also offer a mental health support initiative called the MOMENT program. Applications for most programs can be submitted online, though as of April 2026, paper applications are no longer accepted and clients must either schedule in-person appointments or use online applications with processing times up to 14 business days.

CAPNA distinguishes itself through its holistic, family-centered approach to poverty reduction rather than focusing on single-issue interventions. The organization operates a podcast called 'The Problem with Poverty' that aims to address misconceptions about poverty and poverty-affected individuals. They maintain a volunteer-based model alongside professional services, allowing community members to participate in helping vulnerable populations. The agency serves multiple vulnerable populations including children, seniors, people with disabilities, and working families struggling with utility costs.

As a non-profit community action agency, CAPNA is best suited for low-income individuals and families in their service area seeking free or heavily subsidized assistance with basic needs. The main caveat is that services are subject to income eligibility requirements, appointment availability on a first-come, first-served basis (with waitlists once slots fill), and application processing delays of up to 14 business days. Additionally, the organization's website provides limited detail about specific income thresholds or other program eligibility criteria.
pros: Comprehensive range of free services covering utilities, housing, childcare, meals, and weatherization in one organization
Serves 16 counties across North Alabama, providing broad geographic coverage for the region
No-cost utility assistance and weatherization programs help reduce energy bills and home safety hazards
Free Head Start early childhood programs prepare children for kindergarten at no cost to families
Volunteer opportunities allow community members to serve (Foster Grandparents, Senior Companions programs)
Online application process available for most programs, accessible 24/7 without office visit
Non-judgmental, human-centered approach emphasizing dignity in service delivery
cons: Appointments for utility assistance are offered on first-come, first-served basis with monthly waitlists once full, creating potential access delays
Application processing takes up to 14 business days from assignment date, not submission date, extending wait times for assistance
Website provides limited information about income eligibility thresholds, asset limits, or specific program qualification criteria
Online applications are sometimes closed (as of April 2026), forcing applicants to wait for in-person appointment availability
Geographic service area is limited to specific 16 North Alabama counties, excluding those outside the region
best_for: Low-income families struggling to pay heating or cooling bills who need immediate utility assistance
Working families seeking free or subsidized early childhood care (Head Start) for children before kindergarten
Seniors and people with disabilities needing meal delivery and wellness check-ins through Meals on Wheels
Low-income homeowners seeking weatherization improvements and housing counseling to build financial stability
diagnosis: CAPNA is best for low-income individuals and families in their 16-county North Alabama service area seeking free or subsidized assistance with basic needs like utilities, housing, childcare, and food. The main caveat is that services operate on appointment availability with potential waitlists, applications require up to 14 business days to process, and eligibility requirements are not clearly detailed on their website, necessitating direct contact to determine qualification.
services: Energy Assistance (LIHEAP) - Help paying utility bills when income is insufficient
Weatherization Assistance - Home improvements to reduce energy costs and improve safety
Children's Services/Head Start - Early childhood education and comprehensive childcare services
Housing Counseling and Support Services - Budgeting and housing stability counseling
Affordable Housing Programs - Access to safe, affordable rental or owned homes
Meals on Wheels & More - Home-delivered nutritious meals for seniors and disabled individuals
Senior Companions Program - In-home companionship service to help seniors remain independent
Foster Grandparents Program - Volunteer opportunity for seniors to work in schools with children
Mental Health Support (MOMENT Program) - Motivational outreach and mental wellness guidance
Online appointment scheduling for utility assistance
Application processing and intake for all programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 12529 Al Hwy 157, Suite K, Moulton, AL 35650
